name: David Rose
abstract: David Daniel Rose (June 15, 1910 – August 23, 1990) was a British-American songwriter, composer, arranger, pianist, and orchestra leader. His best known compositions were "The Stripper", "Holiday for Strings", and "Calypso Melody".

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Capp
about_quotes.evidence: I did twelve years on The Red Skelton Show... I did that with David Rose.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Capp interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Don Menza and Sam Noto
about_quotes.evidence: David Rose is mentioned only as a colleague/friend of Henry Mancini ('Pete Rugolo, David Rose and there’s somebody else and Mancini. They were all in Hawaii...') and as someone the interviewee's wife 'Rose' knew ('Rose loved him'). The interviewee does not describe a direct personal or professional
about_quotes.doc_title: Don Menza and Sam Noto interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, January 28, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Broadbent
about_quotes.evidence: I can love charts by David Rose or Percy Faith, you know it’s the orchestration and the music, whatever.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Broadbent interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 26, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Bushkin
about_quotes.evidence: Because when David Rose left Winged Victory I became musical director and I was conducting every night.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Bushkin interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Gerald Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: I joined ASMAC and I felt great, I was in there with guys like David Rose was one of my favorite
about_quotes.doc_title: Gerald Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
